explanations.1: This special comment tells Docker to use a specific syntax version for parsing the Dockerfile, which enables newer Docker features.
explanations.30: The --mount flags here are Docker BuildKit features that cache pip packages and bind-mount the requirements file to avoid copying it into the image layer.
explanations.45: This RUN command uses two mounts: one caches pip's download folder across builds (speeding things up), and one reads requirements.txt without copying it into the image.
explanations.51: The tail command keeps the container running indefinitely; without it, the container would exit immediately after startup.
explanations.65: The backslash at the end continues this single RUN command across multiple lines; Docker treats it all as one command to minimize image layers.
explanations.76: The addgroup command creates a user group (like a team) identified by a numeric ID; this is required before you can create a user that belongs to that group.
explanations.92: This command writes a fancy colored bash prompt into the user's shell config file; it will display each time the user opens a terminal in the container.
explanations.98: This SNIPPET variable stores a bash export command that will be added to .bashrc, making bash automatically save history to a file for persistence.
